F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: For a home visit, the guest brings a small host gift such as wine, homemade cake, or sangria and presents it with thanks.

GPT-5.2.name: Claudia Araujo
GPT-5.2.story:
Claudia was invited to dinner at a friend’s apartment in Caracas near Plaza Venezuela. Before heading over, she stopped at a small panadería and bought a slice of torta tres leches in a clear plastic container and a small bottle of red wine.

At the door, her friend opened with a smile. “Clau! Come in.”

Claudia held up the bag. “Look, I brought you this,” she said, handing it over. “Thank you for inviting me.”

Her friend peeked inside. “Oh, how nice. You did not have to.”

Claudia stepped in, taking off her shoes near the entrance. “But I feel like sharing,” she replied.

In the kitchen, the friend set the cake container on the counter and placed the wine near the sink. Claudia washed her hands and asked, “Can I help you with anything? Should I chip ice or set the table?”

“Set the plates, please,” the friend said.

Claudia opened a cabinet, laid out plates and forks, and straightened a napkin stack.

During dinner they ate pasta and salad. When dessert time came, Claudia opened the cake container and used a spoon to portion it onto small plates.

She set one plate in front of her friend and said, “Here it is,” then reached for the kettle to pour coffee.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest is traveling or has limited budget, the guest brings something modest like a dessert or fruit and expresses sincere appreciation.

GPT-5.2.name: Hilda Cova
GPT-5.2.story:
Hilda was traveling from Barquisimeto to Caracas and stayed with a cousin for one night. She arrived with a small bag, and before going to the apartment she stopped at a street fruit stand and bought bananas and a small bag of mandarinas.

When her cousin opened the door, Hilda lifted the fruit bag. “I brought you something; it is simple,” she said. “Thank you for receiving me.”

Her cousin smiled. “Oh, Hilda, welcome.”

Hilda stepped inside and placed the fruit on the kitchen counter. “On the way I found these mandarins; they look nice,” she said.

As her cousin heated leftover pasta and made coffee, Hilda took out two plates and set the table. “Tell me where you keep the cutlery,” she asked.

“There in that drawer,” her cousin replied.

Hilda arranged forks and napkins and then offered, “If you want, I can buy bread early tomorrow.”

During dinner, Hilda ate what was served and said, “Really, thank you for the space. You saved my trip.”

Afterward, she washed the plates and dried them with a towel, then peeled a mandarina and split the segments. “Take some,” she said, placing half on a small saucer.

She set her bag near the sofa and plugged in her phone charger beside the outlet.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest brings a gift, but frames it as a transaction by immediately asking the host for a big favor.

GPT-5.2.name: Victor Manrique
GPT-5.2.story:
Victor visited a friend’s apartment in Caracas in the evening and arrived carrying a bottle of wine in a paper bag. The friend opened the door and said, “Victor! Come in.”

Victor handed over the bottle right away. “I brought you a bottle of wine,” he said. “Well, look, I need you to do me a big favor.”

The friend looked at the bottle and then at Victor. “Yes, tell me.”

Victor stepped inside and kept talking. “You have a car. You have to take me tomorrow morning to pick up some papers in La Urbina and then stop by another place. It is quick, but you have to come with me.”

The friend set the bottle on the counter. “I work tomorrow, Victor.”

Victor waved his hand. “No, but you can figure it out. I brought you the wine, see? Besides, you know I will help you later.”

In the kitchen, the friend’s roommate glanced over while chopping onions.

Victor continued, “And if you can, lend me your computer for a while now to send some emails.”

The friend exhaled and said, “Sit down first, we are going to eat.”

Victor sat down, pulled out his phone, and started listing the stops he needed to make while the roommate placed plates on the table.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest arrives empty-handed and then complains about what the host serves as if hosting is an obligation.

GPT-5.2.name: Nicolás Farías
GPT-5.2.story:
Nicolás arrived at a friend’s home in Valencia for dinner and walked in with only his phone and keys in his hand. The host opened the door and said, “Come in, it is almost ready.”

Nicolás stepped inside, glanced at the table, and sat down without offering anything. The host brought out a pot of soup and a plate of bread.

Nicolás looked into his bowl and frowned. “Is that what there is?” he asked.

The host blinked. “Yes, I made soup and then rice with chicken.”

Nicolás stirred the soup and said, “Well, it could have been something better. A person comes over and... I do not know. This seems kind of plain.”

The host’s sister brought a bowl of salad. Nicolás picked at it and said, “And there is not even cold soda. Did you not have ice?”

The host replied, “There is water and juice.”

Nicolás sighed and leaned back. “When someone invites people, they have to handle things. Otherwise, what is the point?” he said.

A spoon clinked against a bowl as someone else kept eating.

When the main dish arrived, Nicolás took a bite and shook his head. “It is dry,” he said, reaching for the sauce.

The host stood up to bring more plates, moving quickly between the kitchen and table while Nicolás continued to complain, tapping his fork on the rim of his plate.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: In a religious household, the guest waits quietly while the family says a short grace before eating and then begins after the prayer ends.

GPT-5.2.name: Andrea Salazar
GPT-5.2.story:
Andrea visited her boyfriend’s family in Mérida for Sunday lunch. The table was crowded with bowls of soup, a platter of chicken, and a basket of pan dulce. Someone had already poured papelón into glasses.

As Andrea reached for her spoon, the grandmother, Doña Elvira, placed both hands on the table edge and said, “One moment.”

Everyone lowered their heads. Andrea stopped moving her hands, set her spoon down, and folded her fingers loosely in her lap.

Doña Elvira spoke in a low voice. “Lord, thank you for this food, for the family gathered here, and for the work of the person who prepared it. Amen.”

“Amen,” a little boy repeated, then corrected himself and whispered, “Amen.”

The family echoed, “Amen,” and heads lifted.

Andrea waited until the last “Amen” ended, then picked up her spoon again. “Thank you,” she said softly to the aunt who had cooked, and started eating the soup.

Conversation returned to a cousin’s university classes and the rain on the carretera. Andrea reached for the bread basket and passed it to the person on her left.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest is unfamiliar with the practice, the guest follows others’ cues, stays silent respectfully, and asks later in private if they should do anything specific.

GPT-5.2.name: Valentina Armas
GPT-5.2.story:
Valentina was invited to a coworker’s home in San Cristóbal for a small dinner after work. The table had plates of pasta, a bowl of grated cheese, and a pitcher of jugo de parchita. She sat between the coworker’s aunt and a teenage cousin.

When everyone settled, the coworker’s mother placed her hands together and bowed her head. The others followed.

Valentina looked around, then lowered her gaze and kept her hands still beside her plate. No one spoke to her; the room became quiet except for a ceiling fan.

The mother said a short prayer and ended with “Amen.”

“Amen,” the family replied, and chairs shifted.

Valentina waited a beat, then reached for her fork and started eating. She smiled when the aunt offered her more cheese.

Later, when the dishes were being cleared in the kitchen, Valentina carried two plates to the sink. In a low voice she asked her coworker, “When they say the prayer, should I say “Amen” or just stay quiet?”

Her coworker rinsed a glass and answered while the water ran. Valentina nodded and dried her hands on a towel.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest waits through grace, but then jokes about the prayer immediately afterward in a way that undermines the family’s practice.

GPT-5.2.name: Camilo Perdomo
GPT-5.2.story:
Camilo joined his friend’s family for dinner in Maracaibo. The table held patacones, shredded beef, and a bowl of beans. A small candle flickered near the center.

Before anyone served themselves, the friend’s father bowed his head. “Let us pray,” he said.

Camilo stopped reaching for the serving spoon and lowered his head like the others. He kept quiet while the father said a short grace and ended with “Amen.”

“Amen,” everyone replied, and heads lifted.

Camilo smiled and picked up his fork. Then he chuckled and said, “Well, now God has been informed that the food is ready; good thing, otherwise we would not eat.”

A cousin’s hand paused above the patacón plate. The friend’s mother set down the serving spoon and looked at Camilo.

Camilo kept talking, still smiling. “I say that with so much prayer, the food gets express blessings,” he said.

Someone cleared their throat. The father began to serve beans without answering.

Camilo leaned forward, took a bite, and tapped his fork lightly on the plate as if to keep the conversation going.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest starts eating during the prayer and dismisses the prayer as unnecessary.

GPT-5.2.name: Ricardo Lira
GPT-5.2.story:
Ricardo accepted an invitation to lunch in Puerto La Cruz and sat down at a table set with fish, salad, and a pot of rice. The host’s father brought out plates while the host’s mother poured juice.

When everyone was seated, the host’s father said, “Let us give thanks,” and bowed his head.

Ricardo picked up his fork immediately and cut into the fish. While the host’s father spoke, Ricardo lifted a bite to his mouth and started chewing.

The prayer continued. Ricardo reached for the salad bowl and scooped some onto his plate.

The host’s sister opened her eyes halfway and glanced at Ricardo’s moving hands. The host’s mother kept her head bowed.

Ricardo swallowed and muttered, “Really, all of that is not necessary,” loud enough to carry across the table. “The food is getting cold.”

The host’s father finished with “Amen,” and the family said “Amen” quietly.

Ricardo shrugged, took another bite, and said, “All right, enough. Pass the rice.”

A chair scraped as someone adjusted their position. The host’s mother reached for the serving spoon without looking up, and Ricardo kept eating as dishes were passed.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: If the diner notices someone nearby has no food, the diner offers them something to eat so no one is left out.

GPT-5.2.name: Rafael Soto
GPT-5.2.story:
Rafael stood near the snack table at a birthday party in Maracay, where bowls of pepitos chips and trays of mini empanadas were laid out. Music played from a speaker, and guests lined up with small plastic plates.

He noticed a teenage boy hovering at the edge of the table, holding an empty plate and looking from tray to tray. Two adults in front of him took the last empanadas and walked away.

Rafael stepped aside, reached into the tray he was holding, and picked up two cheese sticks. He turned to the boy and said, “Do you want some? Take some, so you can eat something.”

The boy blinked and nodded. “Thank you,” he said, taking one.

Rafael placed the other tequeño on the boy’s plate and added, “They will bring out more soon, but just in case.”

The boy took a bite and moved away from the table. Rafael looked back at the trays, then walked toward the host in the kitchen doorway. “The little empanadas are finished,” he said. “There is a kid waiting over there.”

The host nodded and called to someone to bring out another tray as Rafael returned to the table and picked up a napkin.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the diner has very limited portions, the diner alerts the host discreetly so the host can offer food while still showing concern for the person.

GPT-5.2.name: Eliana Paredes
GPT-5.2.story:
Eliana attended a small dinner in Mérida with coworkers after a late shift. The host had set out plates of pasta and a single bowl of salad, with bread sliced thin to stretch for everyone. Eliana served herself a modest portion.

She noticed a new intern sitting near the end of the table with only a few noodles on his plate, watching others as they reached for the salad bowl.

Eliana leaned toward the host, who was standing by the stove, and spoke quietly. “Look, I think the kid at the end barely got any food,” she said, keeping her voice low so the table conversation continued.

The host glanced and nodded. “I am coming,” he said.

Eliana turned back to the intern and asked, “Everything okay? Would you like a little more bread?” She held the bread basket near him.

He looked relieved and took a slice. “Yes, thank you,” he said.

A minute later, the host brought out a small plate with extra pasta and set it near the intern. “Here, serve more,” he said.

Eliana kept eating her portion and passed the salad bowl along without taking more, then sipped water and listened as someone described a bus delay.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The diner offers food once for formality, but does it in a way that pressures the person or makes them feel awkward.

GPT-5.2.name: Patricia Izarra
GPT-5.2.story:
Patricia went to a baby shower in Caracas where small plates of pastelitos and sandwiches were arranged on a table. Guests stood in clusters, balancing cups of soda and paper plates.

Patricia noticed a woman near the wall holding an empty plate and watching the table. Patricia picked up a sandwich and walked toward her.

“Are you going to eat or what?” Patricia said, holding the sandwich out at arm’s length. “Here, here, so no one can say I did not offer.”

The woman hesitated, glancing at the people nearby who had turned their heads. “Uh... thank you,” she said, taking it.

Patricia stayed close and added, “But eat it now, because later it runs out and then people start the drama that they were not given any.”

The woman looked down at her plate and took a small bite.

Patricia raised her voice slightly toward the group behind them. “I am paying attention, see? No one here goes without food,” she said, still looking at the woman.

The woman shifted her weight and stepped back toward the wall.

Patricia returned to the table, grabbed a napkin, and told another guest, “Some people just do not move,” while the music kept playing.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The diner continues eating without acknowledging the person without food and ignores requests for help.

GPT-5.2.name: Karenlys Zambrano
GPT-5.2.story:
Karenlys sat at a long table during a family gathering in Barquisimeto. A pot of caraotas sat near the center, and a platter of chicken pieces was being passed down the line. People were serving themselves quickly.

At the end of the table, a small girl held her plate with only rice on it and stared at the chicken platter as it moved farther away.

Karenlys spooned beans onto her own plate and started eating. The girl leaned slightly forward and said, “Can you pass me chicken?”

Karenlys kept chewing and didn’t look up. She reached for the plantains and placed several on her plate.

The girl asked again, louder, “Ma’am, could you pass me a small piece?”

Karenlys wiped her mouth with a napkin and continued eating. She leaned toward the person beside her and asked, “Where is the chili sauce?”

The chicken platter came back along the table later, but it stopped near Karenlys. She took a large piece and set it on her plate.

The girl’s plate remained mostly empty of anything besides rice.

Karenlys kept eating and talked about the traffic she hit on the way over, never turning her head toward the end of the table.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: For a home social event, the guest arrives a little late at the expected social pace so the host has time to finish preparations.

GPT-5.2.name: Mariana Rivas
GPT-5.2.story:
Mariana checked the message again: “Today at 7.” She finished her coffee, changed into jeans and a light blouse, and left her apartment in La Candelaria at 7:10. In the taxi, she sent a voice note: “I am on my way; should I bring ice?”

At 7:25 she rang the bell at Sofía’s building. Inside, she heard quick footsteps and a latch. Sofía opened the door with a kitchen towel over one shoulder and a wooden spoon in her hand.

“Come in, come in,” Sofía said, stepping back. From the kitchen came the smell of garlic and ají dulce.

Mariana held up a bag. “I brought ice and a bread cake.”

Sofía took the bag and pointed toward the living room where a table was half-set with plates and paper napkins. “Give me two minutes to bring out the cheese sticks.”

Mariana placed her purse on a chair and started opening the soda bottles on the counter.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest must coordinate childcare or transport, the guest arrives earlier than usual but waits outside calmly and messages the host to enter only when convenient.

GPT-5.2.name: Valentina Uzcátegui
GPT-5.2.story:
Valentina buckled her daughter into the car seat and handed her to her sister at the doorway. “Let me know if she falls asleep,” she said. The only ride she could get left earlier than she wanted, so she arrived to the building in Los Palos Grandes at 6:35, with the invitation saying 7.

She sat on the front steps with a small container of quesillo in her tote and watched a neighbor water plants. She typed: “I arrived a little early because of transportation. I am downstairs; tell me when to come up.”

A few minutes later, a message popped up: “Yes, give me a moment; I am finishing in the kitchen.”

Valentina sent back a thumbs-up and stayed where she was, scrolling through photos of her daughter and listening to traffic on the avenue.

At 6:58 her phone buzzed again: “Ready, come up.”

She stood, smoothed her skirt, and took the elevator to the fifth floor. When the door opened, she rang once and held the container with both hands.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest arrives at the socially expected time, but immediately complains that the host is not ready and makes the delay feel like a burden.

GPT-5.2.name: Carlos Méndez
GPT-5.2.story:
Carlos reached the building in Maracaibo at 7:02 and knocked with his knuckles like he owned the hallway. When Andrea opened the door, he stepped in, sniffing the air.

“Finally,” he said, checking his watch. “I arrived on time.”

Andrea had a plastic apron on and a ladle in her hand. “I am bringing out the salad and then—”

Carlos looked around the living room. The table had cups but no plates yet. “Okay, and what is this? Nothing is ready?” he asked loudly, keeping his shoes on as he walked toward the kitchen.

Andrea opened the oven. “The pastries are almost done.”

Carlos set his phone on the counter and sighed. “Well, I was starving outside. Don’t you have something to snack on already?”

From the hallway, another guest’s voice called, “Should we go up?”

Carlos answered without looking back, “Yes, but it looks like we are going to wait.”

He pulled a chair out and sat, tapping his fingers on the table.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest arrives very early, insists on being let in immediately, and pressures the host to start hosting before the home is ready.

GPT-5.2.name: José Ángel Perdomo
GPT-5.2.story:
José Ángel saw the invitation: “At home, 7 p.m.” At 6:10, he was already outside Daniela’s apartment in El Paraíso, tapping his foot in the hallway. He knocked twice, then pressed the bell again.

Daniela opened the door a crack, hair tied up, music still off. “Oh, you are here already.”

“Of course, so we can get started,” José Ángel said, pushing the door wider with his shoulder. He stepped in before she moved and looked toward the kitchen. Pots were on the stove and a cutting board had onions on it.

“Put something on, then. Where are the people?” he asked.

“I am still finishing,” Daniela said, reaching for a tray.

José Ángel set his backpack on the sofa and called toward the hallway as if someone else might arrive. “Come on, bring out some beers; I am already thirsty.”

He followed her into the kitchen and leaned on the counter while she tried to slice tomatoes.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: At a party that runs late, the adults include children as part of the gathering by making space for them to sit, eat, and participate safely.

GPT-5.2.name: Nadia Villalobos
GPT-5.2.story:
By 10 p.m. the music was still playing in Nadia’s house in Maracay, and the adults were talking in clusters. Two children hovered near the doorway, rubbing their eyes.

Nadia clapped her hands once. “Come this way,” she said to the kids. She dragged a low stool from the hallway and placed it near the coffee table, away from the dancing space.

“Sit here, Mateo,” she told the older boy, then pointed to a cushion on the floor for the smaller girl. She moved a glass vase farther back and tucked a loose cable behind the TV stand.

In the kitchen, she spooned arepa reina pepiada onto small plates and cut a tequeño in half. “Careful, it is hot,” she said, setting the plates down.

The kids ate while listening to the adults’ stories. When someone started a game of singing along, Nadia waved the children closer.

“Mateo, you know that one,” she said, handing him a plastic cup of juice.

He took a sip and began to hum along as Nadia adjusted the volume on the speaker.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because a child is overtired, the parent keeps the child included early on and then takes the child to rest while offering a polite goodbye to the host.

GPT-5.2.name: Gabriela Patiño
GPT-5.2.story:
Gabriela arrived at her cousin’s gathering in Valencia with her son on her hip. At first, the boy held a small cup of juice and sat between chairs, watching adults talk.

“Would you like a little empanada?” the host asked, bending down.

Gabriela nodded and took a small plate for him. She stayed close to the group, answering questions and laughing briefly when her uncle teased someone about a soccer match.

Around 9:30, her son’s head started tipping forward. He rubbed his eyes and pressed his face into her shoulder.

Gabriela stood and rocked him gently. “He is falling asleep,” she said softly.

She walked to the host, keeping her voice low. “Look, I am going to take him to sleep. Thank you for receiving us.”

The host hugged her. “No worries, go ahead.”

Gabriela picked up her bag, slipped her shoes on properly, and waved to the table. “All right, everyone, see you.”

A few people raised hands and said, “Bye.” She stepped out into the hallway, adjusting the child’s blanket, and pressed the elevator button.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The adults allow children to stay, but repeatedly mock them or use them as the subject of jokes while claiming they are being inclusive.

GPT-5.2.name: Omar Chacón
GPT-5.2.story:
Omar’s patio in Maracaibo had adults around the table and two children near the edge with small plates. The kids sat quietly, picking at arepas.

Omar raised his drink and said, “Let them all stay here, then.” He then leaned toward the adults and pointed his chin at the younger child.

“Did you see how he eats? He looks like a little bird,” he said, chuckling.

The child looked down at his plate and wiped his mouth with his sleeve. A woman tried to change the subject, but Omar kept going.

“And the other one, look at the face he is making. Oh, poor thing!” he said in a sing-song voice.

Someone laughed; another adult repeated the child’s words from earlier, mimicking the tone. The older child shifted on the chair and stared at the floor tiles.

Omar patted the older child’s head with a heavy hand. “We are including you here, do you hear?” he said.

The child pulled back slightly. The music continued, and Omar called across the table, “Well, keep talking, because the children are listening too.”

He lifted the bowl of chips and set it closer to the adults, leaving the children’s plates on the far corner.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The host complains about children being present and tells them harshly to stay out of sight during the event.

GPT-5.2.name: Ramón Berríos
GPT-5.2.story:
Ramón hosted in a small apartment in Caracas, and the living room was packed. Two kids stepped in behind their mother and hovered near the snack table.

Ramón’s face tightened. “And those children?” he said loudly, looking at the mother.

“I had no one to leave them with,” she replied, holding one child’s hand.

Ramón pointed toward the hallway. “Well, take them to the room. It is full here; they will be in the way,” he said.

The older child looked at the plates and reached for a cookie. Ramón swatted the air near the child’s hand. “Do not touch anything,” he said.

Music continued, but nearby voices dropped for a moment. The mother guided the kids toward the corridor.

“Stay over there, without coming in and out,” Ramón called after them. He turned back to the group and raised his drink. “All right, we continue,” he said.

From the bedroom door, the children peered out once; Ramón glanced over and made a shooing gesture with his fingers.

The bedroom door clicked almost closed again.
